description: Rewrite the evolution orchestrator (scripts/evolution-loop.sh) as a new Rust crate `evolution-loop` in the Cargo workspace, eliminating bash/Python, adding typed state, per-phase timeouts, and structured logging.

userStories.title: Create evolution-loop crate scaffold
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a new `evolution-loop` Rust crate added to the Cargo workspace so that the orchestrator can be built like any other crate.
acceptanceCriteria: Directory `evolution-loop/` created with valid `Cargo.toml` (name = "evolution-loop", binary target named `evolution-loop`)
`evolution-loop` added to `members` array in root `Cargo.toml`
Dependencies declared: serde (features=["derive"]), serde_json, clap (features=["derive"]), anyhow, chrono (features=["serde"]), tracing, tracing-subscriber (features=["env-filter"]), ctrlc
`evolution-loop/src/main.rs` exists with a `fn main()` that prints "evolution-loop" and exits 0
`cargo build -p evolution-loop` compiles without errors
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement CLI with start and resume subcommands
userStories.description: As an operator, I want a clear CLI with `start` and `resume` subcommands so that I can start new sessions and resume interrupted ones.
acceptanceCriteria: Subcommand `start` accepts: --baseline-version <tag> (required), --ideas-file <path> (optional), --output-dir <path> (default: tasks/evolution-runs), --max-iterations <n> (default: 10), --max-infra-failures <n> (default: 3), --phase-timeout-secs <n> (default: 1800), --verbose (flag)
Subcommand `resume` accepts: --session <path> (required), --from <phase> (optional, one of: propose/implement/validate/benchmark/decide), --phase-timeout-secs <n> (default: 1800), --verbose (flag)
`evolution-loop --help` prints usage and exits 0
`evolution-loop start --help` and `evolution-loop resume --help` each print subcommand-specific help
Missing required `--baseline-version` in `start` prints a descriptive error and exits non-zero
Unknown arguments print a descriptive error and exit non-zero
`cargo build -p evolution-loop` compiles without errors
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement typed structs for session and iteration state
userStories.description: As a developer, I want strongly-typed Rust structs for all session/iteration state so that JSON manipulation bugs are caught at compile time.
acceptanceCriteria: Module `state` (or `types`) defines `SessionMetadata` struct with Serialize/Deserialize covering all fields currently in `session.env`: baseline_version, active_baseline_version, active_baseline_path, active_baseline_binary, accepted_baseline_version, accepted_baseline_path, accepted_baseline_binary, accepted_baseline_major, accepted_baseline_minor, candidate_version, candidate_binary_path, ideas_file, ideas_file_pending_count, ideas_format, stockfish_binary, max_iterations, max_infra_failures, session_id, session_dir, summary_file

Module defines `IterationState` struct with Serialize/Deserialize covering all top-level fields in `iteration.json`: iteration, baselineVersion, baselinePath, baselineBinary, ideas (IdeasState), candidate (CandidateState), state (IterationPhase), isolation (IsolationState), correctness (CorrectnessState), stockfishComparison (StockfishComparisonState), stateMachine (StateMachineState), artifacts (ArtifactsState), decision (Option<DecisionState>)
Enum `IterationPhase` covers: initialized, proposing, proposed, implementing, implemented, validating, benchmarking, benchmarked, deciding, accepted, rejected, inconclusive, failed
Function `transition_phase(state: &mut IterationState, from: IterationPhase, to: IterationPhase) -> anyhow::Result<()>` returns Err if current state != from
Function `load_iteration_state(path: &Path) -> anyhow::Result<IterationState>` reads and deserializes iteration.json
Function `save_iteration_state(path: &Path, state: &IterationState) -> anyhow::Result<()>` serializes with 2-space indent and trailing newline
Function `load_session_metadata(path: &Path) -> anyhow::Result<SessionMetadata>` reads key=value file
Function `save_session_metadata(path: &Path, meta: &SessionMetadata) -> anyhow::Result<()>` writes key=value file
Unit test: round-trip serialize/deserialize of an `IterationState` value produces identical JSON
Unit test: `transition_phase` returns Err when called with wrong `from` state
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement version tag parsing and candidate versioning
userStories.description: As the orchestrator, I want version tag parsing and candidate version computation so that candidate versions are correctly bumped based on proposal source.
acceptanceCriteria: Function `parse_version_tag(tag: &str) -> anyhow::Result<(u32, u32)>` parses `vMAJOR.MINOR` and returns Err for invalid format
Function `candidate_version_for_source(baseline: &str, source: ProposalSource) -> anyhow::Result<String>` returns minor bump for self_proposed, major bump for user_ideas_file
Function `cargo_semver_from_tag(tag: &str) -> anyhow::Result<String>` returns `MAJOR.MINOR.0`

Function `apply_candidate_manifest_versions(workspace: &Path, semver: &str) -> anyhow::Result<()>` updates `version = "..."` in chess-engine/Cargo.toml, chess-runner/Cargo.toml, chesslib/Cargo.toml using string replacement (one replacement per file, error if none found)
Enum `ProposalSource` with variants `SelfProposed` and `UserIdeasFile`

Unit tests: parse_version_tag("v1.2") == Ok((1,2)); parse_version_tag("bad") == Err; candidate_version_for_source("v1.2", SelfProposed) == "v1.3"; candidate_version_for_source("v1.2", UserIdeasFile) == "v2.0"
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement git worktree management
userStories.description: As the orchestrator, I want candidate git worktrees to be created and cleaned up reliably so that interrupted sessions don't leave orphaned worktrees.
acceptanceCriteria: Function `create_candidate_workspace(repo_root: &Path, candidate_dir: &Path, baseline_ref: &str) -> anyhow::Result<()>` shells out to `git worktree add --detach <dir> <ref>` then `git -C <dir> checkout -b <branch>`
Function `remove_candidate_workspace(repo_root: &Path, candidate_dir: &Path)` shells out to `git worktree remove --force <dir>` then `rm -rf <dir>`; does not panic/error if directory does not exist
Function `candidate_branch_name(session_id: &str, iteration: u32) -> String` returns `wiggum-evolution/<session_id>/iteration-<n>`
Function `candidate_has_uncommitted_changes(candidate_dir: &Path) -> anyhow::Result<bool>` checks `git diff --quiet` and `git diff --cached --quiet` and untracked files
Function `commit_candidate_workspace(candidate_dir: &Path, iteration: u32) -> anyhow::Result<()>` runs `git add -A` then `git commit -m "chore: accept evolution iteration <n>"`
All git commands capture stdout and stderr; on non-zero exit code return Err with the captured stderr included in the error message
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement correctness gate (validate phase)
userStories.description: As the orchestrator, I want a correctness gate that runs cargo build and cargo test in the candidate workspace and records results before benchmarking.
acceptanceCriteria: Function `run_correctness_gate(candidate_dir: &Path, iteration_state_path: &Path, correctness_results_path: &Path, timeout_secs: u64) -> anyhow::Result<CorrectnessOutcome>` runs two commands sequentially in `candidate_dir`: (1) `cargo build --workspace`, (2) `cargo test --workspace -- --skip gen_files::magics::name`
Each command has a per-command timeout equal to `timeout_secs`; if it exceeds the timeout, the process is killed and the check is marked failed with reason "timeout"
Returns `CorrectnessOutcome::Passed` if both pass, `CorrectnessOutcome::Failed(Vec<CheckResult>)` otherwise
Writes `correctness/results.md` with status (passed/failed) and per-check results
Updates `iteration.json` correctness field with status, passed bool, benchmarkEligible bool, and checks array
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement Claude phase execution with timeout and logging
userStories.description: As the orchestrator, I want each Claude skill phase to run with a configurable timeout and produce a captured log so that hung processes don't block the session.
acceptanceCriteria: Function `run_phase(config: &PhaseConfig) -> anyhow::Result<PhaseOutcome>` spawns `$CLAUDE_BIN --dangerously-skip-permissions --add-dir <session_dir> --add-dir <repo_root> --print` with a heredoc-style stdin containing `/<skill_name>\n\n<context>` as the prompt, cwd set to candidate workspace
CLAUDE_BIN defaults to `openclaude`, overridable via environment variable
If the process exceeds `phase_timeout_secs`, it is killed with SIGKILL and `PhaseOutcome::Timeout` is returned
In quiet mode: stdout+stderr of the Claude process are captured to `phase-logs/<phase>.log` only
In verbose mode: stdout+stderr are written simultaneously to `phase-logs/<phase>.log` AND to the orchestrator's stdout using a background reader thread (tee behavior)

The phase prompt context includes: repository root, session directory, iteration directory, iteration state file path, session metadata file path, worker guidance path, and all guidance text from the current `run_claude_skill` function in `scripts/evolution-loop.sh`
Function returns `PhaseOutcome::Success` on exit code 0, `PhaseOutcome::Failed(exit_code)` otherwise
`PhaseConfig` struct holds: skill_name, candidate_workspace, iteration_dir, iteration_state_path, session_dir, repo_root, session_metadata_path, phase_timeout_secs, verbose
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement ideas file checklist management
userStories.description: As the orchestrator, I want the ideas file checklist to be updated after each tested iteration so that ideas aren't retried.
acceptanceCriteria: Function `count_pending_ideas(path: &Path) -> anyhow::Result<u32>` counts lines matching `^\s*-\s*\[ \]\s+.+` in the ideas file

Function `mark_idea_used(ideas_path: &Path, selected_idea: &str) -> anyhow::Result<MarkResult>` finds the first unchecked entry matching `selected_idea` (trimmed), replaces `[ ]` with `[x]`, and returns `MarkResult::Updated`, `MarkResult::NotFound`, or `MarkResult::Skipped` (when path is empty or proposal_source != user_ideas_file)
Function `resolve_ideas_file(raw_path: &str, repo_root: &Path) -> anyhow::Result<Option<PathBuf>>` resolves relative/absolute path, returns None if file has zero pending ideas
After `mark_idea_used`, `ideas_file_pending_count` is updated in session metadata
If `pending_count` reaches 0 after marking, `ideas_file` field is cleared in session metadata so self-propose flow kicks in
Unit test: count_pending_ideas counts only `- [ ]` entries, not `- [x]` entries
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement candidate promotion and artifact persistence
userStories.description: As the orchestrator, I want accepted candidates to be atomically promoted as the new baseline so that subsequent iterations use the correct binary.
acceptanceCriteria: Function `persist_promoted_artifact(candidate_binary: &Path, promoted_version: &str, repo_root: &Path) -> anyhow::Result<PathBuf>` copies binary to `chess-engine/versions/<promoted_version>/wiggum-engine`, sets executable bit, returns the destination path

Function `promote_candidate(candidate_dir: &Path, iteration_state_path: &Path, session_meta: &mut SessionMetadata, repo_root: &Path) -> anyhow::Result<()>` reads promoted version and candidate binary from `iteration.json`, calls `persist_promoted_artifact`, updates `session_meta` active and accepted baseline fields, writes `iteration.json` with promotion fields filled
Function `baseline_version_path(repo_root: &Path, version: &str) -> PathBuf` returns `<repo_root>/chess-engine/versions/<version>`
If the candidate binary file does not exist at promotion time, return Err with a descriptive message
After successful promotion, `active_baseline_version`, `active_baseline_path`, `active_baseline_binary` in `SessionMetadata` are updated to the promoted version
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement session summary writer
userStories.description: As an operator, I want a human-readable summary.md written at session end so that I can audit what happened.
acceptanceCriteria: Function `write_session_summary(path: &Path, session_dir: &Path, session_id: &str, baseline_version: &str, max_iterations: u32, completed_iterations: u32, stop_reason: &str, stop_reason_details: &str, accepted_baseline_version: &str, accepted_baseline_path: &str) -> anyhow::Result<()>` writes summary.md

Summary includes: session id, baseline inicial, baseline final aceito, max iterations, completed iterations, session directory, stop reason, stop details, list of accepted versions, list of rejected attempts, per-iteration artifact links (iteration.json, hypothesis.md, implementation.md, correctness/results.md, benchmark.md, stockfish-comparison/results.md, decision.md)
Reads all `iterations/<n>/iteration.json` files to build the per-iteration table
Writes a placeholder summary at session start (`Status: pending final session summary`)
Overwrites placeholder with final summary at session end
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement iteration artifact initialization
userStories.description: As the orchestrator, I want all iteration artifacts initialized before phases run so that skills find the expected files.
acceptanceCriteria: Function `create_iteration_artifacts(session_dir: &Path, iteration: u32, session_meta: &SessionMetadata, candidate_workspace: &Path, candidate_branch: &str, candidate_setup_status: &str, candidate_setup_error: &str) -> anyhow::Result<IterationPaths>` creates directories and writes initial files
Creates: `iterations/<n>/`, `iterations/<n>/correctness/`, `iterations/<n>/stockfish-comparison/`, `iterations/<n>/phase-logs/`
Writes placeholder markdown for: hypothesis.md, implementation.md, correctness/results.md, benchmark.md, stockfish-comparison/results.md, decision.md

Writes `iterations/<n>/iteration.json` with all fields populated matching the structure currently produced by `write_iteration_state` in the shell script, including stockfishComparison, stateMachine, artifacts with full paths, phase log paths
If candidate workspace setup failed, `iteration.json` is pre-populated with state=failed, correctness.status=skipped, benchmark.status=skipped, decision.outcome=failed
Returns `IterationPaths` struct with paths to all key files for use by the iteration runner
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement main iteration loop and session initialization
userStories.description: As an operator, I want the main loop to run up to --max-iterations iterations with all stopping conditions so that sessions terminate gracefully.
acceptanceCriteria: Session `start` flow: validates baseline version format, resolves baseline artifacts (checks directory and binary exist), generates session ID as `YYYYMMDDTHHMMSSz` via chrono, creates session directory, writes placeholder summary and initial session.env, then runs the iteration loop

Iteration loop runs from 1 to max_iterations; each iteration: creates artifacts → sets up worktree → runs propose → runs implement → runs validate → copies binary → runs benchmark → runs decide → handles outcome
After each phase, reads `iteration.json` to verify expected state transition; if state is wrong, calls record_phase_failure and continues to next iteration

Stopping conditions: (a) max iterations reached, (b) infra failure count >= max_infra_failures, (c) propose phase sets hypothesis.status=no_hypothesis (stop_reason = "no valid next hypothesis could be generated"), (d) unexpected iteration state
After `accepted` outcome: marks idea used, promotes candidate, removes worktree; resets infra_failure_count on success, increments on failure
After `rejected` or `inconclusive`: marks idea used, removes worktree, resets infra_failure_count (increments only if mark_idea_used returns NotFound)
After `failed` outcome: increments infra_failure_count, removes worktree
Writes final summary.md after loop exits regardless of stop reason
Prints startup banner with session config, and per-iteration progress lines matching current shell script output style
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement resume mode
userStories.description: As an operator, I want to resume an interrupted session at a specific phase so that I don't have to restart from scratch.
acceptanceCriteria: Session `resume` flow: reads session.env from --session path, loads session metadata, resolves accepted baseline artifacts, finds latest iteration number from `iterations/` directory

If --from is not specified, infers resume phase from `iteration.json.state` using the same mapping as the shell script (initialized→propose, proposing→propose, proposed→implement, implementing→implement, implemented→validate, benchmarking→benchmark, benchmarked→decide, deciding→decide)
Validates that the candidate workspace directory still exists and that baselinePath/baselineBinary referenced in iteration.json still exist; if not, logs error and marks iteration as failed
Executes only the phases from the resume phase onward (skips earlier phases)
After the resumed iteration completes, handles outcome (acceptance/rejection/failure) identically to the main loop
Does NOT start a new iteration after resuming — run one iteration and stop
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement structured logging and signal handling
userStories.description: As a developer debugging a hang, I want timestamped structured logs and graceful SIGINT handling so that sessions terminate cleanly and are diagnosable.
acceptanceCriteria: tracing-subscriber initialized at startup with format including timestamp (chrono-based), level, and target; default level INFO, overridable via RUST_LOG env var
Every phase start/end logged at INFO with fields: iteration=N, phase=<name>, outcome=<state>
Phase timeout events logged at WARN with fields: iteration=N, phase=<name>, timeout_secs=N
Git and cargo command invocations logged at DEBUG with the full command arguments
SIGINT/SIGTERM handler registered via `ctrlc` crate: on signal, sets a global AtomicBool stop flag; the main loop checks this flag between iterations and writes the partial summary before exiting
In verbose mode, a clear prefix (e.g., `[evolution-loop]`) is added to orchestrator log lines to distinguish them from Claude process output
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Delete scripts/evolution-loop.sh and update references
userStories.description: As the team, I want the shell script removed and all references updated so that the Rust binary is the canonical orchestrator.
acceptanceCriteria: `scripts/evolution-loop.sh` is deleted from the repository
The correctness gate in the new Rust binary no longer checks `bash -n scripts/evolution-loop.sh` (remove that check from the correctness gate, keep only cargo build and cargo test)
Any reference to `scripts/evolution-loop.sh` in CLAUDE.md, README files, or other scripts is updated to reference `./target/debug/evolution-loop` or `./target/release/evolution-loop`
`cargo build --workspace` still compiles successfully after deletion
Typecheck passes
